question: How does SCF community voting work — what is Neural Quorum Governance and who can vote?

golden.answer:
A grounded answer derives its named examples and status claims from the live Stellar Scout directory or research data, then dates the roster so it is not presented as permanent. As of 2026-07-10, the verified sources supported this observed answer:

NQG is Stellar Community Fund's reputation/delegation governance system, combining Neural Governance with Quorum Delegation; it is not XLM/token voting and not Stellar Consensus Protocol. The current four-tier permissions matter: **Verified** has identity/account verification and view/discussion access; **Pathfinder** may participate by delegating weight; **Navigator** and **Pilot** may vote actively on Build Open projects; **Pilot** may also serve as a delegate and act on governance proposals. Build project-level community voting applies to the **Open Track only**. Integration and RFP awards are panel-led. Public Goods and quarterly resource-governance votes also use NQG-related signals but are distinct vote objects with different eligibility/processes.

The dated example is illustrative: a later grounded answer should report what the same source basis supports at its own observation time while preserving the stable distinctions above.
keyFacts: Presents specific source-supported content as a current or dated observation rather than as an unsupported memory claim.
Makes the as-of date visible for every changeable roster, status, version, or measurement.
Separates Build Open voting from Integration/RFP decisions and from Public Goods/quarterly governance.
Explains NQG as reputation/delegation governance, not token voting or SCP.
avoid: Do NOT say every basic Verified member actively votes or can be a delegate.
Do NOT generalize Build Open voting to Integration/RFP or treat Public Goods as the same vote.
Do NOT present the observed live result set as a complete or permanent roster; explicitly historical facts remain historical.
golden.notes:
Dossier §3. Key trap: NQG (SCF grant governance) vs SCP quorum sets (protocol consensus) — different things despite both saying 'quorum'.
Also good if the answer: NQG was adopted by SCF in October 2023 and was co-developed with BlockScience. | The community vote applies to the Build Open Track (and the Public Goods Award).
Golden cites: The SCF handbook Neural Quorum Governance / Verified Members pages.
GT-39/40 atom independently confirmed 2026-07-10. Grade the role matrix and vote-object boundary, not the shorthand 'Verified members vote'.
